content:
And it’s much faster

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image by author from canvaFree member? Read here —
Microsoft has announced TypeScript 7.0.
Instead of building on the existing JavaScript codebase, the Microsoft dev team rebuilt, yes, rebuilt the compiler in Go.
And the result?
Projects that once took minutes to build can now finish in just a few seconds.
What does this mean to developers?

• Faster project builds.

• Quicker error checking.

• Near-instant auto-completion.

• A much smoother coding experience.
They claim it to be a 10x faster native port of TypeScript!
Memory usage has also gone down, making the compiler more efficient.
Some big tech giants like Microsoft, Google, Slack, Canva, Figma, Notion, and Vercel have worked on the latest update and given feedback too.
